channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Gary Gensler said he’ll step down as SEC chair. Bitcoin is soaring
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are ecstatic after U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler said he will step down from his post on January 20 once President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: 2025 will be the year of the CIO and CMO collaboration
item.description: Same executive board, different roles. Historically, that’s been how CIOs and CMOs work and operate across a company. But as we close out 2024, this siloed approach must come to an end.
item.dc:creator: Keith Turco, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin is at the doorstep of $100,000 as post-election rally rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin topped $98,000 for the first time Thursday, extending a streak of record after record highs since the U.S. presidential election. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SONiC pioneer Aviz Networks raises $17M for AI network management push
item.description: Networking software provider Aviz Networks today announced a $17 million Series A funding round to accelerate its growth in open networking solutions and artificial intelligence capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Thai Court Dismisses Lawsuit Against Israeli Spyware Firm
item.description: Thai court throws out lawsuit against NSO Group over alleged use of Pegasus spyware
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Australia's parliament considers legislation banning social media for under 16s
item.description: Legislation for the world's first legal ban on children under 16 using social media has been introduced in Australia's Parliament.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace
item.description: Modernizing space programs while maintaining operations is one of the most challenging things, and rings true for the U.S. Space Force. The post How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: What to do when you hate your favorite influencers’ politics
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: DoD, Hill eye CMMC tax credit for smaller defense contractors
item.description: The CMMC tax credit would be available to companies with 50 or fewer employees. The bill could feature in negotiations over tax cuts next year. The post DoD, Hill eye CMMC tax credit for smaller defense contractors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's disturbing message
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump watches as Musk's SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Trump joined Elon Musk on Tuesday in Texas to observe the launch of a SpaceX rocket as the two leaders have spent significant time together since Election Day. Trump was joined by some of his staff and Republican lawmakers, including Sens. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Germany blames ‘sabotage’ as two undersea fiber cables cut in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The German government has blamed an act of sabotage for the cutting of two important undersea fiber optic cables, one connecting connecting Finland and Germany, and the other linking Sweden and Lithuania, on Sunday and Monday. The first cable break happened at around 8 a.m. GMT (3 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Explore the evolution of zero trust in government The post Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Designing the APIs that accidentally power businesses
item.description: In the last decade, every web application developer has become an API designer. But most organizations do not think of developers as API designers, nor do they think about APIs as a product of design — to the detriment of their developers’ productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to make your VPN faster
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how a VPN — virtual private network — works and how running it can slow down the operation of your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network
